creator: tz@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to townwear from comment #10)

>However, if each version of pecl-memcached is present, you can easily install it 
> with pkg without having to use cumbersome ports.
> It is also great for server administrators.

But it doesn't work this way. Even if i add php71-memcache you can't install - for example - databases/phpmyadmin. Because phpmyadmin is also build against the default PHP version which is 5.6 at the moment. Same for wordpress, joomla, magento, typo 3, etc...

So this will only work for a very particular set of ports - namely when you do not want to install any other port with registered PHP dependency. 

The best (and currently correct way for you) would be setting /etc/make.conf and build them. Or create your own repo with poudriere or synth.
